title: useNetStack Documentation
description: This useNetStack composable provides an abstraction for making API requests with additional features such as retries, caching, timeouts, cancellation, and logging. It also includes a global configuration that can be customized.

children.value: composable provides an abstraction for making API requests with additional features such as retries, caching, timeouts, cancellation, and logging. It also includes a global configuration that can be customized.
, first import the composable into your Vue 3/Nuxt 3 project.
I will add support for other JS/TS applications later in the future as a
At the moment, it is tested and works the best with NUXT 3 applications.
composable allows you to make API calls with customizable options such as retries, timeouts, cache handling, and more.
object defines the structure of the API request.

props.code:
interface ApiRequest {
  method: "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "DELETE" | "PATCH"; // HTTP method

  endpoint: string; // API endpoint

  headers?: Record<string, string>; // Optional headers

  queryParams?: Record<string, string | number>; // Optional query parameters

  body?: any; // Optional body for POST, PUT, etc.
}

children.value:
interface ApiRequest {
  method: "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "DELETE" | "PATCH"; // HTTP method

  endpoint: string; // API endpoint

  headers?: Record<string, string>; // Optional headers

  queryParams?: Record<string, string | number>; // Optional query parameters

  body?: any; // Optional body for POST, PUT, etc.
}

props.code:
interface ExecuteCallParams {
  apiRequest: ApiRequest; // The API request object

  async?: boolean; // Run asynchronously without waiting for the ongoing request

  override?: boolean; // Override any ongoing request for the same endpoint

  retries?: number; // Number of retry attempts on failure (default: 3)

  retryDelay?: number; // Delay between retries in milliseconds (default: 1000ms)

  cancellationToken?: AbortController; // Custom cancellation token

  timeout?: number; // Request timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)

  cacheDuration?: number; // Cache duration in milliseconds (default: 60000ms)

  skipCache?: boolean; // Skip cache and force a fresh request (default: false)
}

children.value:
interface ExecuteCallParams {
  apiRequest: ApiRequest; // The API request object

  async?: boolean; // Run asynchronously without waiting for the ongoing request

  override?: boolean; // Override any ongoing request for the same endpoint

  retries?: number; // Number of retry attempts on failure (default: 3)

  retryDelay?: number; // Delay between retries in milliseconds (default: 1000ms)

  cancellationToken?: AbortController; // Custom cancellation token

  timeout?: number; // Request timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)

  cacheDuration?: number; // Cache duration in milliseconds (default: 60000ms)

  skipCache?: boolean; // Skip cache and force a fresh request (default: false)
}
has a global configuration that can be updated using
. This allows setting default behaviors for all API calls.
supports automatic retries when a network request fails. The number of retries and delay between retries can be configured.
: You can override the global retries for individual requests.
You can set a timeout for requests. If the request takes longer than the specified time, it will be aborted.
: You can override the global timeout for individual requests.
Responses can be cached to prevent redundant network requests within a specific time frame.
Abort controllers can be used to cancel ongoing requests. You can pass a custom
Logging helps in monitoring the network stack’s behavior, such as request retries, errors, caching, and other events.
Performs the API call with retries, caching, timeouts, and other options.

props.code:
async function executeCall({
  apiRequest,

  async,

  override,

  retries,

  retryDelay,

  cancellationToken,

  timeout,

  cacheDuration,

  skipCache,
}: ExecuteCallParams): Promise<any>;

Updates the global configuration of the network stack.
Logging helps trace the flow of requests and catch potential issues during retries, caching, or timeouts. The logging system provides three levels:
: General information about requests, retries, and caching.
: Warnings such as retries or request aborts.
POST Request with Retries and Timeout
